name: 8. 중학 가정법 200
description: 단순조건 vs 가정법, 가정법 과거/과거완료/혼합, I wish, as if, without/but for, if 도치, It's time + 가정법 예문 200개 (중학 수준)

words.w: If it rains, I will stay home.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 비가 오면 집에 있을 것이다. (단순조건: 현재형 + will)

words.w: If he comes, we will leave.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 그가 오면 우리는 떠날 것이다. (단순조건)

words.w: If you study, you will pass.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 공부하면 합격할 것이다. (단순조건)

words.w: If she calls, tell her I'm busy.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 그녀가 전화하면 내가 바쁘다고 말해 줘. (단순조건)

words.w: If you don't hurry, you'll be late.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 서두르지 않으면 늦을 것이다. (단순조건)

words.w: I will help you if I can.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 할 수 있으면 도울 것이다. (단순조건)

words.w: If the weather is nice tomorrow, we'll go hiking.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 내일 날씨가 좋으면 하이킹 갈 것이다. (단순조건)

words.w: If you eat too much, you'll feel sick.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 너무 많이 먹으면 속이 안 좋을 것이다. (단순조건)

words.w: She will be happy if you come.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 네가 오면 그녀가 행복할 것이다. (단순조건)

words.w: If I have time, I will visit you.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 시간 있으면 너를 방문할 것이다. (단순조건)

words.w: If I were a bird, I would fly.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 내가 새라면 날 것이다. (가정법 과거: 현재 반대)

words.w: If I had money, I would buy it.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 내게 돈이 있다면 살 것이다. (가정법 과거)

words.w: If I knew, I would tell you.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 안다면 네게 말해 줄 텐데. (가정법 과거)

words.w: If she were here, she would help.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 그녀가 여기 있다면 도울 것이다. (가정법 과거)

words.w: If he were taller, he could play basketball.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 그가 더 크다면 농구를 할 수 있을 텐데. (가정법 과거)

words.w: If I had wings, I would fly to you.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 내게 날개가 있다면 너에게 날아갈 텐데. (가정법 과거)

words.w: If you studied harder, you would pass.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 더 열심히 공부한다면 합격할 텐데. (가정법 과거)

words.w: If she had time, she would come.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 그녀가 시간이 있다면 올 텐데. (가정법 과거)

words.w: If we lived in Paris, we would be happy.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 파리에 산다면 행복할 것이다. (가정법 과거)

words.w: If I were you, I would accept it.
words.m: [단순조건 vs 가정법] 내가 너라면 받아들일 텐데. (가정법 과거)

words.w: If I were rich, I would travel the world.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 내가 부자라면 세계를 여행할 텐데. (If + were, would + 원형)

words.w: If he were here, he would help us.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 그가 여기 있다면 우리를 도울 텐데.

words.w: If I had a car, I would drive to school.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 차가 있다면 학교에 운전해 갈 텐데.

words.w: If she spoke English, she could work abroad.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 영어를 한다면 해외에서 일할 수 있을 텐데.

words.w: If I knew his number, I would call him.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 그의 번호를 안다면 전화할 텐데.

words.w: If it were sunny, we would go to the beach.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 해가 난다면 해변에 갈 텐데.

words.w: If you tried harder, you would succeed.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 더 열심히 노력한다면 성공할 텐데.

words.w: If I were a teacher, I would be kind.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 내가 선생님이라면 친절할 텐데.

words.w: If we had more time, we would travel more.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 시간이 더 있다면 더 여행할 텐데.

words.w: If he were my friend, I would trust him.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 그가 내 친구라면 그를 믿을 텐데.

words.w: If I lived near you, I would visit often.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 네 근처에 산다면 자주 방문할 텐데.

words.w: If she had a phone, I could text her.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 그녀에게 폰이 있다면 문자할 수 있을 텐데.

words.w: If I were you, I wouldn't do that.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 내가 너라면 그렇게 안 할 것이다.

words.w: If it rained, I would stay inside.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 비가 온다면 안에 있을 텐데.

words.w: If he exercised, he would be healthier.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 그가 운동한다면 더 건강할 텐데.

words.w: If I won the lottery, I'd be rich.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 복권에 당첨된다면 부자가 될 텐데.

words.w: If she were older, she could drive.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 그녀가 더 나이 들었다면 운전할 수 있을 텐데.

words.w: If I had wings, I would fly away.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 날개가 있다면 날아갈 텐데.

words.w: If he listened, he would understand.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 그가 듣는다면 이해할 텐데.

words.w: If I spoke Chinese, I would move to China.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 중국어를 한다면 중국으로 이사 갈 텐데.

words.w: If we had a bigger house, we'd invite more friends.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 더 큰 집이 있다면 친구를 더 초대할 텐데.

words.w: If I weren't busy, I'd join you.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 바쁘지 않다면 합류할 텐데.

words.w: If she studied more, she'd pass.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 그녀가 더 공부한다면 합격할 텐데.

words.w: If I could fly, I'd visit you every day.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 날 수 있다면 매일 너를 방문할 텐데.

words.w: If he were nicer, people would like him.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 그가 더 친절하다면 사람들이 그를 좋아할 텐데.

words.w: If I had a car, I wouldn't walk.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 차가 있다면 걷지 않을 텐데.

words.w: If she were here, we could start.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 그녀가 여기 있다면 시작할 수 있을 텐데.

words.w: If it were summer, we'd go swimming.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 여름이라면 수영하러 갈 텐데.

words.w: If we lived together, it would be fun.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 함께 산다면 재미있을 텐데.

words.w: If I knew the answer, I'd tell you.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 답을 안다면 네게 말해 줄 텐데.

words.w: If he had a bike, he would come faster.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 자전거가 있다면 더 빨리 올 텐데.

words.w: If she were taller, she'd be a model.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 더 크다면 모델이 될 텐데.

words.w: If I weren't tired, I would go out.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 피곤하지 않다면 나갈 텐데.

words.w: If we had a map, we wouldn't get lost.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 지도가 있다면 길을 잃지 않을 텐데.

words.w: If it were warmer, I'd swim.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 더 따뜻하다면 수영할 텐데.

words.w: If he earned more, he'd buy a house.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 더 번다면 집을 살 텐데.

words.w: If I were the president, I'd change things.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 내가 대통령이라면 변화시킬 텐데.

words.w: If she didn't work, she'd be bored.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 그녀가 일하지 않는다면 지루할 것이다.

words.w: If I had a brother, I'd be happy.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 형이 있다면 행복할 텐데.

words.w: If we could speak another language, we could travel freely.
words.m: [가정법 과거] 다른 언어를 할 수 있다면 자유롭게 여행할 텐데.

words.w: If I had known, I would have called.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 알았다면 전화했을 텐데. (If + had p.p., would have p.p.)

words.w: If he had come, we would have waited.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그가 왔다면 우리는 기다렸을 텐데.

words.w: If she had studied, she would have passed.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그녀가 공부했다면 합격했을 텐데.

words.w: If it hadn't rained, we would have gone out.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 비가 안 왔다면 나갔을 텐데.

words.w: If I had had money, I would have bought it.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 돈이 있었다면 샀을 텐데.

words.w: If he had left earlier, he wouldn't have missed the bus.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 일찍 떠났다면 버스를 안 놓쳤을 텐데.

words.w: If she had listened, she wouldn't have been lost.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 들었다면 길을 잃지 않았을 텐데.

words.w: If we had trained harder, we would have won.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 더 훈련했다면 이겼을 텐데.

words.w: If I had been there, I would have helped.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 거기 있었다면 도왔을 텐데.

words.w: If he had told me, I would have come.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 말해 줬다면 갔을 텐데.

words.w: If you had asked, I would have answered.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 물었다면 답했을 텐데.

words.w: If she had known, she would have warned us.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그녀가 알았다면 경고했을 텐데.

words.w: If I hadn't been busy, I would have joined.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 바쁘지 않았다면 합류했을 텐데.

words.w: If we had saved money, we could have traveled.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 돈을 모았다면 여행했을 텐데.

words.w: If she had arrived sooner, we wouldn't have missed the start.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그녀가 더 일찍 왔다면 시작을 놓치지 않았을 텐데.

words.w: If I had seen you, I would have said hi.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 너를 봤다면 인사했을 텐데.

words.w: If they had practiced, they would have won.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그들이 연습했다면 이겼을 텐데.

words.w: If she had called, I would have picked her up.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그녀가 전화했다면 데리러 갔을 텐데.

words.w: If I had read the book, I could have answered.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그 책을 읽었다면 답할 수 있었을 텐데.

words.w: If he had exercised, he would have been fit.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그가 운동했다면 건강했을 텐데.

words.w: If it had snowed, we would have built a snowman.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 눈이 왔다면 눈사람을 만들었을 텐데.

words.w: If you had come earlier, you could have met him.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 네가 일찍 왔다면 그를 만날 수 있었을 텐데.

words.w: If she had taken the medicine, she would have felt better.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 약을 먹었다면 나아졌을 텐데.

words.w: If I had studied French, I could have worked in France.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 프랑스어를 공부했다면 프랑스에서 일할 수 있었을 텐데.

words.w: If he hadn't lied, we wouldn't have been angry.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그가 거짓말하지 않았다면 우리는 화나지 않았을 텐데.

words.w: If we had left earlier, we would have arrived on time.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 더 일찍 떠났다면 제시간에 도착했을 텐데.

words.w: If I had remembered, I would have brought it.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 기억했다면 가져왔을 텐데.

words.w: If she had worn a coat, she wouldn't have caught a cold.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 코트를 입었다면 감기에 안 걸렸을 텐데.

words.w: If he had won, he would have celebrated.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그가 이겼다면 축하했을 텐데.

words.w: If they had helped, we would have finished.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그들이 도왔다면 끝냈을 텐데.

words.w: If I had listened to him, I wouldn't have failed.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그의 말을 들었다면 실패하지 않았을 텐데.

words.w: If we had taken a map, we wouldn't have gotten lost.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 지도를 가져갔다면 길을 잃지 않았을 텐데.

words.w: If she had tried, she could have done it.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그녀가 시도했다면 할 수 있었을 텐데.

words.w: If I had been faster, I would have caught the train.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 더 빨랐다면 기차를 탔을 텐데.

words.w: If we had stayed longer, we could have seen more.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 더 오래 머물렀다면 더 볼 수 있었을 텐데.

words.w: If he had called me, I would have been there.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 그가 내게 전화했다면 거기 있었을 텐데.

words.w: If you had asked for help, I would have helped.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 네가 도움을 청했다면 도왔을 텐데.

words.w: If she had known English, she could have traveled.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 영어를 알았다면 여행했을 텐데.

words.w: If I had saved, I could have bought a house.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 모았다면 집을 살 수 있었을 텐데.

words.w: If we had waited longer, we would have met him.
words.m: [가정법 과거완료] 더 오래 기다렸다면 그를 만났을 텐데.

words.w: If I had studied harder, I would be a doctor now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 더 열심히 공부했다면 지금 의사일 텐데. (과거 조건 + 현재 결과)

words.w: If he had listened to me, he wouldn't be in trouble now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 내 말을 들었다면 지금 곤란에 처하지 않을 텐데.

words.w: If she hadn't missed the bus, she would be here now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 버스를 놓치지 않았다면 지금 여기 있을 텐데.

words.w: If I had saved money, I would be rich now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 돈을 모았다면 지금 부자일 텐데.

words.w: If we had taken that job, we would live in Japan now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 그 일을 맡았다면 지금 일본에 살 텐데.

words.w: If he had eaten breakfast, he wouldn't be hungry now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 아침을 먹었다면 지금 안 배고플 텐데.

words.w: If she had gone to college, she would have a better job now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 대학에 갔다면 지금 더 좋은 직장을 가질 텐데.

words.w: If I had been born earlier, I would be retired now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 더 일찍 태어났다면 지금 은퇴했을 텐데.

words.w: If he had married her, they would be happy now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 그녀와 결혼했다면 지금 행복할 텐데.

words.w: If we hadn't met, I wouldn't know you now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 우리가 만나지 않았다면 지금 너를 모를 텐데.

words.w: If she had learned to drive, she would be here by now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 운전을 배웠다면 지금쯤 여기 있을 텐데.

words.w: If I had taken the offer, I would own a company now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 그 제안을 받아들였다면 지금 회사를 갖고 있을 텐데.

words.w: If he hadn't dropped out, he would be a doctor now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 그만두지 않았다면 지금 의사일 텐데.

words.w: If I had listened to my parents, I wouldn't be here now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 부모님 말을 들었다면 지금 여기 있지 않을 텐데.

words.w: If we had bought that house, we would be living there now.
words.m: [혼합 가정법] 그 집을 샀다면 지금 거기 살고 있을 텐데.

words.w: I wish I were rich.
words.m: [I wish] 내가 부자라면 좋겠다. (I wish + 과거형: 현재 반대)

words.w: I wish I had studied harder.
words.m: [I wish] 더 열심히 공부했으면 좋았을 텐데. (I wish + had p.p.: 과거 후회)

words.w: I wish she were here.
words.m: [I wish] 그녀가 여기 있으면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish I could fly.
words.m: [I wish] 날 수 있으면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish I knew the answer.
words.m: [I wish] 답을 안다면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish I hadn't said that.
words.m: [I wish] 그런 말을 하지 않았다면 좋을 텐데.

words.w: I wish he were my brother.
words.m: [I wish] 그가 내 형이면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish I had more time.
words.m: [I wish] 시간이 더 있으면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish it would stop raining.
words.m: [I wish] 비가 그쳤으면 좋겠다. (would + 원형)

words.w: I wish you were here.
words.m: [I wish] 네가 여기 있으면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish I had gone with them.
words.m: [I wish] 그들과 함께 갔다면 좋을 텐데.

words.w: I wish she spoke English.
words.m: [I wish] 그녀가 영어를 하면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish I could play the piano.
words.m: [I wish] 피아노를 칠 수 있으면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish I had listened.
words.m: [I wish] 들었다면 좋을 텐데.

words.w: I wish it were summer.
words.m: [I wish] 여름이면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish I could help you.
words.m: [I wish] 너를 도울 수 있으면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish he would call.
words.m: [I wish] 그가 전화해 주면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish I hadn't eaten so much.
words.m: [I wish] 그렇게 많이 먹지 않았다면 좋을 텐데.

words.w: I wish I lived closer.
words.m: [I wish] 더 가까이 살면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish she understood me.
words.m: [I wish] 그녀가 나를 이해하면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish I had saved money.
words.m: [I wish] 돈을 모았다면 좋을 텐데.

words.w: I wish we were together.
words.m: [I wish] 우리가 함께 있으면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish I could travel more.
words.m: [I wish] 더 여행할 수 있으면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish he would listen.
words.m: [I wish] 그가 들어주면 좋겠다.

words.w: I wish I had known you earlier.
words.m: [I wish] 너를 더 일찍 알았다면 좋을 텐데.

words.w: He talks as if he were rich.
words.m: [as if] 그는 마치 부자인 것처럼 말한다. (as if + 과거형: 현재 반대)

words.w: She acts as if she knew everything.
words.m: [as if] 그녀는 모든 것을 아는 것처럼 행동한다.

words.w: He looks as if he had seen a ghost.
words.m: [as if] 그는 유령을 본 것처럼 보인다. (as if + had p.p.: 과거 반대)

words.w: She smiled as if she were happy.
words.m: [as if] 그녀는 행복한 것처럼 미소 지었다.

words.w: He walks as if he were the king.
words.m: [as if] 그는 왕인 것처럼 걷는다.

words.w: She speaks as if she knew my secret.
words.m: [as if] 그녀는 내 비밀을 아는 것처럼 말한다.

words.w: It looks as if it is going to rain.
words.m: [as if] 비가 올 것 같다. (실제 가능성 있음, 직설법)

words.w: He treats me as if I were his brother.
words.m: [as if] 그는 나를 형제처럼 대한다.

words.w: She laughs as if she had heard a joke.
words.m: [as if] 그녀는 농담을 들은 것처럼 웃는다.

words.w: He acts as if nothing happened.
words.m: [as if] 그는 아무 일도 없었던 것처럼 행동한다.

words.w: She dresses as if she were a queen.
words.m: [as if] 그녀는 여왕인 것처럼 옷을 입는다.

words.w: He spoke as if he were an expert.
words.m: [as if] 그는 전문가인 것처럼 말했다.

words.w: She acts as if she were in charge.
words.m: [as if] 그녀는 책임자인 것처럼 행동한다.

words.w: He looks as if he hadn't slept.
words.m: [as if] 그는 자지 않은 것처럼 보인다.

words.w: She acts as if we were old friends.
words.m: [as if] 그녀는 우리가 오랜 친구인 것처럼 행동한다.

words.w: He speaks as if he had been there.
words.m: [as if] 그는 거기 가 봤던 것처럼 말한다.

words.w: She laughs as if she were a child.
words.m: [as if] 그녀는 아이처럼 웃는다.

words.w: He treats the baby as if it were his own.
words.m: [as if] 그는 그 아기를 자기 아기인 것처럼 대한다.

words.w: She looks as if she were sad.
words.m: [as if] 그녀는 슬픈 것처럼 보인다.

words.w: He talks as if he had won the lottery.
words.m: [as if] 그는 복권에 당첨된 것처럼 말한다.

words.w: Without water, we couldn't live.
words.m: [without/but for] 물이 없다면 우리는 살 수 없을 것이다. (= if it were not for)

words.w: Without your help, I would have failed.
words.m: [without/but for] 너의 도움이 없었다면 나는 실패했을 것이다. (= if it had not been for)

words.w: But for the rain, we would have gone out.
words.m: [without/but for] 비가 아니었다면 우리는 나갔을 텐데.

words.w: Without air, plants would die.
words.m: [without/but for] 공기가 없다면 식물은 죽을 것이다.

words.w: But for his advice, I would have made a mistake.
words.m: [without/but for] 그의 조언이 아니었다면 나는 실수했을 것이다.

words.w: Without her support, I couldn't do this.
words.m: [without/but for] 그녀의 지원이 없다면 나는 이것을 할 수 없을 것이다.

words.w: Without the sun, there would be no life.
words.m: [without/but for] 태양이 없다면 생명이 없을 것이다.

words.w: But for you, I would be alone.
words.m: [without/but for] 네가 아니었다면 나는 혼자였을 것이다.

words.w: Without his effort, we wouldn't have won.
words.m: [without/but for] 그의 노력이 없었다면 우리는 이기지 못했을 것이다.

words.w: But for her warning, we would have been in danger.
words.m: [without/but for] 그녀의 경고가 아니었다면 우리는 위험에 처했을 것이다.

words.w: Were I rich, I would travel.
words.m: [if 도치] 내가 부자라면 여행할 텐데. (= If I were rich, If 생략+도치)

words.w: Had I known, I would have told you.
words.m: [if 도치] 알았다면 말했을 텐데. (= If I had known)

words.w: Were she here, we could start.
words.m: [if 도치] 그녀가 여기 있다면 시작할 수 있을 텐데.

words.w: Had he studied, he would have passed.
words.m: [if 도치] 그가 공부했다면 합격했을 텐데.

words.w: Should you need help, call me.
words.m: [if 도치] 혹시 도움이 필요하면 전화해라. (= If you should need)

words.w: Were it not for her, I'd be lost.
words.m: [if 도치] 그녀가 아니라면 나는 헤맬 텐데. (= If it were not for)

words.w: Had it not been for him, we'd have failed.
words.m: [if 도치] 그가 아니었다면 우리는 실패했을 것이다.

words.w: Were I in your shoes, I would quit.
words.m: [if 도치] 내가 네 입장이라면 그만둘 텐데.

words.w: Had she listened, she would have known.
words.m: [if 도치] 들었다면 알았을 텐데.

words.w: Should he call, tell him I'm busy.
words.m: [if 도치] 혹시 그가 전화하면 바쁘다고 말해 줘.

words.w: It's time you went to bed.
words.m: [It's time] 너 벌써 잠자리에 들어야 할 시간이다. (It's time + 과거형)

words.w: It's time we left.
words.m: [It's time] 우리 이제 떠나야 할 시간이다.

words.w: It's time he got a job.
words.m: [It's time] 그가 직장을 구해야 할 시간이다.

words.w: It's time you studied harder.
words.m: [It's time] 너 더 열심히 공부해야 할 시간이다.

words.w: It's time we cleaned the house.
words.m: [It's time] 우리가 집을 청소해야 할 시간이다.

words.w: It's time they left.
words.m: [It's time] 그들이 떠나야 할 시간이다.

words.w: It's time she made a decision.
words.m: [It's time] 그녀가 결정해야 할 시간이다.

words.w: It's high time you apologized.
words.m: [It's time] 네가 진작 사과해야 했다. (high time = 강조)

words.w: It's about time he came.
words.m: [It's time] 이제야말로 그가 와야 한다. (about time)

words.w: It's time we took a break.
words.m: [It's time] 우리 쉬어야 할 시간이다.

words.w: Should you change your mind, let me know.
words.m: [기타-도치·관용] 혹시 마음이 바뀌면 알려 줘. (Should + 주어)

words.w: Had I not met you, my life would be different.
words.m: [기타-도치·관용] 너를 만나지 않았다면 내 삶이 달랐을 것이다.

words.w: Suppose you won the lottery, what would you do?
words.m: [기타-도치·관용] 복권에 당첨된다고 가정하면 뭘 할 거니? (suppose = if)

words.w: If only I could see her again.
words.m: [기타-도치·관용] 그녀를 다시 볼 수만 있다면. (If only = I wish 강조)

words.w: If only I had known earlier!
words.m: [기타-도치·관용] 더 일찍 알았다면!

words.w: I'd rather you didn't go.
words.m: [기타-도치·관용] 네가 가지 않기를 바란다. (would rather + 주어 + 과거형)

words.w: I'd rather she stayed.
words.m: [기타-도치·관용] 그녀가 남기를 바란다.

words.w: If only he would listen.
words.m: [기타-도치·관용] 그가 들어주면 좋겠다.

words.w: Suppose it rains, what then?
words.m: [기타-도치·관용] 비가 온다고 치면 그때는 어떻게?

words.w: Imagine you were famous.
words.m: [기타-도치·관용] 네가 유명하다고 상상해 봐.
